ai_summary.one_liner: Second-generation embodied foundation model achieving 99% task success rates at 3x speed with only 1 hour of task-specific robot data
ai_summary.problem: First-gen embodied models like GEN-0 achieved ~64% success rates — not commercially viable for real-world deployment
ai_summary.solution: GEN-1: scaled pretraining on 500K+ hours of human wearable data (no robot data), redesigned multimodal architecture, RL-based post-training, and inference-time optimization with harmonic reasoning
ai_summary.key_result: 99% success rates across 6 tasks (vs. GEN-0's 64%), 3x faster execution, 10x less task-specific fine-tuning data needed
limitations: Not all attempted tasks reach mastery-level performance
Emergent improvisation behaviors can be liabilities when misaligned with task objectives
No open-source model or dataset release

cover_caption: GEN-1: Scaling Embodied Foundation Models to Mastery — the first general-purpose physical AI crossing commercial viability with 99% success rates.
source_title: GEN-1: Scaling Embodied Foundation Models to Mastery

figures.caption: GEN-1: Scaling Embodied Foundation Models to Mastery — Open Graph hero image from the Generalist AI blog announcement.
figures.caption_ko: GEN-1: Embodied 파운데이션 모델의 숙련도 스케일링 — Generalist AI 블로그 공개 히어로 이미지.
